_note: Los Angeles HQ data — SoFi Stadium, Inglewood. 5 group stage matches + 2 Round of 32 + a Quarterfinal.

venue.name: SoFi Stadium
venue.notes: Expanded to roughly 100,000 for World Cup matches. Clear bag policy strictly enforced. The translucent roof and open sides mean it still feels outdoors — dress for SoCal sun even on 'indoor' matchdays.
venue.parkingNote: On-site Hollywood Park lots from $75+, often sold out weeks ahead. Book via the official FIFA app or Ticketmaster. Most fans take Metro.

transit.recommended: LA Metro K Line to Downtown Inglewood station — about a 15-minute walk or free shuttle to SoFi Stadium
lines: K Line — Downtown Inglewood station, direct walk/shuttle to the stadium plaza
C Line (Green) — Hawthorne/Lennox station, transfer to free game-day shuttle
tips: Buy a TAP card before match day — K Line platforms get crowded fast pre-kickoff.
I-405/I-105 interchange near the stadium is one of the busiest in the country — avoid driving if at all possible.
Hollywood Park plaza (The Forum side) is the main pre- and post-match gathering area.
Last trains run later on match nights, but confirm timing for late kickoffs before you head in.
transit.parking: Hollywood Park lots $75–$150+ on match days, often sold out in advance. Remote lots with shuttle service are cheaper but add travel time.
transit.rideshare: Designated Uber/Lyft pickup near Pylon Plaza on the stadium's west side. Expect long surge windows after evening kickoffs given the 405/105 bottleneck.

fanZones.name: FIFA Fan Festival — Exposition Park
fanZones.location: Exposition Park, near USC, Downtown LA
fanZones.notes: Free entry, giant screens and live music near the Coliseum. Location based on early planning reports — confirm with official FIFA channels closer to kickoff.

fanZones.name: Hollywood Park Plaza
fanZones.notes: The retail/entertainment plaza around SoFi fills with supporters' groups well before kickoff — restaurants, bars, and big screens right at the gates.

preGame.name: Hollywood Park / The Forum district
preGame.notes: New restaurants and bars built around SoFi and The Forum — the easiest pre-game option since you're already at the venue.

preGame.name: Downtown Inglewood (Market Street)
preGame.notes: Local restaurants and bars along Market Street fill up with fans riding in on the K Line — more affordable than the stadium district.

preGame.name: Manhattan Beach / South Bay
preGame.notes: Beach-town watch party scene for fans wanting to combine the World Cup with the Pacific coast before heading to SoFi.

cityEnergyForecast.crowdNote: USA vs Paraguay — the United States' tournament opener at SoFi Stadium. Expect a sold-out, red-white-and-blue crowd from the first whistle.
Iran vs New Zealand — Group G Matchday 3 with no US team involved. Smaller but passionate diaspora crowds on a Monday evening.
Switzerland vs Bosnia-Herzegovina — Group B opener with two well-traveled European fan bases. Solid midweek noon crowd.
Belgium vs Iran — Group G opener. Belgium's golden-generation pedigree draws a strong neutral turnout for a Sunday noon kickoff.
Türkiye vs USA — Group D finale with the hosts' knockout-stage fate on the line. The biggest matchday of LA's group stage by far.

transitPain.label: Very High
transitPain.note: USA vs Paraguay — Friday evening kickoff for the host nation's opener. The 405/105 interchange will be gridlocked for hours around SoFi.
tips: Take the K Line — driving to SoFi on opener night is not recommended
Arrive at Downtown Inglewood station 90+ minutes before kickoff
Post-match, expect long waits for trains and rideshares alike — pack patience

transitPain.label: Moderate
transitPain.note: Iran vs New Zealand — Monday evening kickoff overlaps with rush hour on the 405/105, but stadium crowds will be lighter than USA matches.
tips: K Line still the easiest option during rush hour
Lighter crowd means shorter lines at Downtown Inglewood station
Good day to scout your route for busier matchdays

transitPain.label: Low
transitPain.note: Switzerland vs Bosnia-Herzegovina — Thursday noon kickoff avoids both rush hour and a USA-sized crowd.
tips: Best day to drive and park if you must — lots won't sell out
K Line trains run comfortably outside peak commute hours
Combine with Hollywood Park plaza for an easy pre-match lunch

transitPain.label: Low
transitPain.note: Belgium vs Iran — Sunday noon kickoff means light weekend traffic on the 405/105 corridor.
tips: Weekend midday is one of the easiest windows to reach SoFi all tournament
K Line and C Line both run normal weekend schedules — no rush-hour overlap
Good opportunity to explore Downtown Inglewood before the match

transitPain.label: Very High
transitPain.note: Türkiye vs USA — Thursday evening Group D decider with the hosts' fate on the line. Expect SoFi opener-level gridlock again.
tips: Treat this like the opener — K Line only, arrive 90+ minutes early
Hollywood Park plaza will be at capacity well before kickoff
Pre-book any post-match transport; rideshare surge will be extreme if the US needs a result

shouldIGoScores.label: Don't miss it
shouldIGoScores.summary: USA vs Paraguay — the United States' opening match of their home World Cup, at SoFi Stadium. This is the ticket of LA's group stage, full stop.

shouldIGoScores.label: Consider
shouldIGoScores.summary: Iran vs New Zealand — a quieter Group G fixture with no US team involved. Good value and an easier matchday if you want SoFi without the opener crush.

shouldIGoScores.label: Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: Switzerland vs Bosnia-Herzegovina — competitive Group B football with two committed European fan bases, plus the most relaxed matchday logistics of LA's schedule.

shouldIGoScores.label: Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: Belgium vs Iran — Belgium's experienced core is always worth watching, and a Sunday noon kickoff makes this one of the easiest SoFi matchdays to attend.

shouldIGoScores.label: Don't miss it
shouldIGoScores.summary: Türkiye vs USA — the Group D finale with the hosts' knockout-stage destiny on the line. If the US needs a result, SoFi Stadium will be the loudest building in the country.
